reasoning: The flagged eval usage in wasm_runtime.js is contextually safe - it operates in browser runtime and isn't executed during AUR build/install. No risks found in PKGBUILD or build hooks.
disagreement_analysis: Qwen misclassified wasm_runtime.js as unsafe due to eval usage without considering it only runs in browser contexts, not during AUR operations.
coverage_issues: Both models skipped perry-0.5.1182.tar.gz binary (acceptable for size)
Qwen didn't review critical test scripts (run_tests.sh, run_parity_tests.sh) that DeepSeek validated
false_positives: Qwen's wasm_runtime.js verdict: eval usage is constrained to same-origin iframes in browser runtime, not executed during makepkg
learnings: Improve context-awareness: Models should prioritize files referenced in PKGBUILD (build/check/install hooks) over general source code
Enhance eval detection: Differentiate between browser-context eval (often safe) vs. build-time eval (high-risk)
Refine file selection: Test scripts should be higher priority if referenced in PKGBUILD check() function
